title: plans/okay-i-want-you-breezy-duckling.md embeds hardcoded Infisical project ID and production Logfire base URL
body_excerpt: `plans/okay-i-want-you-breezy-duckling.md` (lines 30–35) embeds a hardcoded Infisical project ID (`9ec75f86-d604-4cfa-902c-57cb1e372adc`) and a production Logfire base URL in a bash snippet.
evidence: plans/okay-i-want-you-breezy-duckling.md line 32 still contains `--projectId 9ec75f86-d604-4cfa-902c-57cb1e372adc` and line 36 has `export LOGFIRE_BASE_URL=https://logfire-eu.pydantic.dev`. The plans/ directory is not in .gitignore. Both the credential and the URL remain committed to the repo.
suggested_notion_title: Security: Infisical project ID and Logfire URL hardcoded in plans/okay-i-want-you-breezy-duckling.md
suggested_notion_body: plans/okay-i-want-you-breezy-duckling.md (line 32) still contains hardcoded Infisical project ID 9ec75f86-d604-4cfa-902c-57cb1e372adc and Logfire production base URL (line 36). The plans/ directory is not in .gitignore. Fix: either redact the values and rotate if sensitive, or add plans/ to .gitignore and remove the directory. Per project Secrets Management rules, access parameters belong in Doppler.

title: plans/ directory has byte-identical duplicate plan files (scalable-mixing-naur, swift-juggling-wadler)
body_excerpt: `plans/bright-dazzling-yeti.md`, `plans/scalable-mixing-naur.md`, and `plans/swift-juggling-wadler.md` are byte-identical (md5: `75dcc8e44660b54ef212d9f43ebfe2a2`). All three are 315-line copies of the same Stage 3 planning content.
evidence: plans/scalable-mixing-naur.md and plans/swift-juggling-wadler.md no longer exist in the working tree. Only plans/bright-dazzling-yeti.md remains. The duplicate files were removed.

title: Known Exceptions section stripped from CLAUDE.md — sessions.ts drizzle-orm exception removed
body_excerpt: The `Known Exceptions` section was significantly stripped. Removed:
evidence: CLAUDE.md Known Exceptions section exists but does NOT contain the sessions.ts drizzle-orm import exception or the grep verification command (`grep -lE "from 'drizzle-orm'" apps/api/src/routes/*.ts`). The mobile→api tsconfig reference exception IS present. The sessions.ts exception was removed and has not been restored. Sessions.ts itself no longer imports drizzle-orm (confirmed), but the documentation gap remains — future cleanup agents lack the bounded exception context.
suggested_notion_title: Docs: CLAUDE.md Known Exceptions missing sessions.ts drizzle-orm annotation (grep verification command)
suggested_notion_body: CLAUDE.md Known Exceptions section is missing the sessions.ts drizzle-orm import exception and its grep verification command. This exception was removed in PR #217. Although sessions.ts no longer imports drizzle-orm (route is clean), the documentation gap means future agents cannot confirm the exception scope. Restore the annotation with the grep command or confirm it is no longer needed.

title: Known Exceptions: sessions.ts drizzle-orm grep verification command removed
body_excerpt: 1. The `sessions.ts` drizzle-orm import exception, including the grep verification command (`grep -lE "from 'drizzle-orm'" apps/api/src/routes/*.ts`) that proves it is the only site.
evidence: CLAUDE.md does not contain the grep verification command `grep -lE "from 'drizzle-orm'" apps/api/src/routes/*.ts`. The exception detail including the bounded verification command was removed in PR #217 and not restored. The mobile→api tsconfig exception is still documented. This is the same root gap as the finding above.

title: Known Exceptions: apps/mobile/tsconfig.json→apps/api reference exception with rationale removed
body_excerpt: 2. The `apps/mobile/tsconfig.json` → `apps/api` reference exception with rationale. `CLAUDE.md` retains both in full.
evidence: CLAUDE.md Known Exceptions section still contains the apps/mobile/tsconfig.json → apps/api reference exception with full rationale including the AppType import context and the type-only vs runtime import distinction. This exception was preserved.

title: append-followup.sh: doppler guard runs before NOTION_API_KEY env-var check making env-var path unreachable on non-doppler machines
body_excerpt: The `command -v doppler` guard at line 73 runs **before** the `NOTION_API_KEY` env-var check at line 87. Any Archon node that injects `NOTION_API_KEY` into the environment but runs without the doppler binary will still fail at line 73.
evidence: .archon/scripts/append-followup.sh lines 84-91: the NOTION_API_KEY environment check (`if [[ -n "${NOTION_API_KEY:-}" ]]`) now runs first; the `command -v doppler` guard is inside the `else` branch. Comment on line 80-81 explicitly documents this fix: 'The doppler binary check is gated inside the else branch so machines that have NOTION_API_KEY set but no doppler installed still succeed (AW-003).'

title: execute-cleanup-pr.yaml filename vs name: execute-cleanup-pr-merged mismatch
body_excerpt: File: AW-004 Line: LOW Issue: `execute-cleanup-pr.yaml` on disk vs `name: execute-cleanup-pr-merged` — verify Archon discovers by filename or `name:` field before activating.
evidence: .archon/workflows/execute-cleanup-pr.yaml in the main repo has `name: execute-cleanup-pr` matching its filename. The mismatch (name: execute-cleanup-pr-merged) only exists in the route-qsv-migration worktree copy, which is not the canonical source. The main repo file is consistent.

title: model: gpt-5.5 in DRAFT workflow is unverified against OpenAI model registry
body_excerpt: File: AW-005 Line: LOW Issue: `model: gpt-5.5` in the DRAFT workflow is unverified against OpenAI model registry. Inline comment filed.
evidence: .archon/workflows/execute-cleanup-pr-claude.yaml and execute-cleanup-pr-codex.yaml both still contain `model: gpt-5.5`. The execute-cleanup-pr.yaml workflow also contains multiple `model: gpt-5.5` references. No change has been made to verify or update this model identifier.
suggested_notion_title: Config: gpt-5.5 model identifier unverified in Archon workflow files
suggested_notion_body: Multiple .archon/workflows/ files (execute-cleanup-pr.yaml, execute-cleanup-pr-claude.yaml, execute-cleanup-pr-codex.yaml) specify `model: gpt-5.5`. This model identifier has not been verified against the OpenAI model registry. If gpt-5.5 does not exist, Archon invocations will fail silently or error. Verify the correct model name and update accordingly.

title: gather-review-context.sh diff cache has no commit-hash suffix — could be stale if HEAD moves mid-run
body_excerpt: File: AW-002 Line: LOW Issue: `gather-review-context.sh` diff cache has no commit-hash suffix — could be stale if HEAD moves mid-run. Confirm Archon always uses a fresh `artifacts_dir` per run.
evidence: .archon/scripts/gather-review-context.sh line 31: `_diff_cache="${artifacts_dir}/.diff"` — no commit-hash suffix. If the script is invoked twice in the same artifacts_dir with HEAD at different commits, the cached diff will be stale for the second invocation. No commit-hash was added to the cache filename.
suggested_notion_title: Archon: gather-review-context.sh diff cache stale if HEAD moves mid-run (no commit-hash suffix)
suggested_notion_body: In .archon/scripts/gather-review-context.sh, the diff cache path is `${artifacts_dir}/.diff` with no commit-hash suffix. If Archon reuses the same artifacts_dir across multiple workflow runs with HEAD at different commits, the second run will use a stale diff. Fix: append `$(git rev-parse HEAD)` to the cache filename, e.g. `${artifacts_dir}/.diff-$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)`.

title: HTML dispatch graph is stale on arrival — PR-18 shown as inprog but cleanup-plan.md marks it done
body_excerpt: **HTML dispatch graph is stale on arrival** (`docs/audit/pr-dispatch-graph.html`): The file shows PR-18 as `inprog` in both the wave card (line 288) and the status table (line 481), but the `cleanup-plan.md` changes in this same PR mark PR-18 as done
evidence: docs/audit/pr-dispatch-graph.html no longer exists in the main repo's docs/audit/ directory. The file was moved to docs/_archive/consistency-cleanup/audit/pr-dispatch-graph.html where it now shows PR-18 with `class="done"` in the wave card and `DONE` in the status table. The stale inprog state described in the finding is no longer present.

title: Unquoted $options in for loop in cleanup-extract.sh subject to pathname expansion
body_excerpt: **Unquoted `$options` in `for` loop is subject to pathname expansion** (`.archon/scripts/cleanup-extract.sh`): With `IFS=','`, bash word-splits on commas correctly — but it also performs glob expansion on each split token before the loop body runs.
evidence: .archon/scripts/cleanup-extract.sh uses `IFS=',' read -ra _opts <<< "$options"` followed by `for opt in "${_opts[@]}"` — the exact `read -ra` fix suggested by the finding. The file's comment at line 57-64 explicitly documents this change: 'Split on commas WITHOUT pathname expansion. Plain `for opt in $options` word-splits AND glob-expands each token ... `read -ra` with IFS=',' splits on commas only.'

title: .test-receipts/mobile.json has hand-crafted --runTestsByPath command excluding failing OCR test suite
body_excerpt: **File:** `.test-receipts/mobile.json` The `command` field in the receipt is a manually composed jest invocation with `--runTestsByPath` restricting the run to a hand-picked subset of files. `record-test-receipt.sh` **always** writes `command: "pnpm exec nx run mobile:test"`
evidence: The entire .test-receipts/ system was removed in commit 8c4173a03 ('chore(test): remove mobile receipt gate'). Files deleted: .test-receipts/.gitkeep, .test-receipts/mobile.json, scripts/record-test-receipt.sh, scripts/verify-test-receipts.sh. The receipt gate infrastructure no longer exists in the repo, eliminating the antipattern.

title: .test-receipts/mobile.json hand-crafted --runTestsByPath cannot evidence full suite passing
body_excerpt: `.test-receipts/mobile.json` records a hand-crafted `--runTestsByPath` invocation over 9 specific files. The `scripts/record-test-receipt.sh` script always runs `pnpm exec nx run mobile:test` (full suite).
evidence: Same root cause as the Critical finding above. The .test-receipts/ infrastructure was fully removed in commit 8c4173a03. Both the handcrafted receipt and the record-test-receipt.sh script no longer exist.

title: .agents/skills/commit/SKILL.md: opt-out push model conflicts with explicit-confirmation principle
body_excerpt: **File:** `.agents/skills/commit/SKILL.md` The change rewrites: "Never push unless the user explicitly says 'push' or 'commit and push'." to: "Push after successful commits unless the user explicitly says not to push."
evidence: .agents/skills/commit/SKILL.md rule 1 currently reads: 'Push after successful commits unless the user explicitly says not to push. Never force-push unless explicitly requested.' The opt-out-of-push model introduced by PR #268 remains in place. The original opt-in model ('Never push unless user explicitly says push') has not been restored.
suggested_notion_title: Security/UX: commit skill auto-pushes by default — opt-out model vs opt-in principle
suggested_notion_body: apps/.agents/skills/commit/SKILL.md rule 1 uses opt-out push semantics: 'Push after successful commits unless the user explicitly says not to push.' This conflicts with the principle that actions visible to others require explicit confirmation. Auto-push pushes WIP commits and shared PR branches without user signal. Consider reverting to opt-in: 'Never push unless the user explicitly says push or commit and push', or scope auto-push to a flag/separate skill.

title: ChatShell.test.tsx: new internal jest.mock() calls without gc1-allow (lines 32, 60, 77, 89)
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/session/ChatShell.test.tsx: all 4 new internal mocks have // gc1-allow: annotations on the jest.mock() call line itself (lines 40, 69, 87, 100). External boundary mocks (expo-router, @react-navigation/native, react-native-safe-area-context, @expo/vector-icons) have no annotation as required. The gc1-allow placement is correct per CLAUDE.md.

title: use-sessions.test.ts: new internal jest.mock() calls without gc1-allow (lines 26, 51, 60)
body_excerpt: File: `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-sessions.test.ts` Line: 3 (excl. `sse` which the new test directly depends on) Issue: 26, 51, 60
evidence: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-sessions.test.ts: all 3 internal mocks (api-client line 28, api line 54, profile line 65) have // gc1-allow: annotations on the jest.mock() call line. The sse mock (line 59) also has gc1-allow. Annotations are correctly placed per CLAUDE.md requirements.

title: gc1-allow comments placed inside factory body instead of on jest.mock() call line (lines 9, 16, 23)
body_excerpt: The three `// gc1-allow:` comments are placed as the first line inside the factory body, not on the `jest.mock(` call line as CLAUDE.md requires. Affects lines 9, 16, and 23.
evidence: In apps/mobile/src/components/session/ChatShell.test.tsx, all gc1-allow annotations appear on the same line as jest.mock() (e.g., line 40: `jest.mock('../../lib/math-format', () => ({ // gc1-allow: ...`). No gc1-allow comments appear as the first line inside factory bodies. The placement is correct per CLAUDE.md.

title: handleSend in ChatShell.tsx short-circuits on !isFocused for all platforms but editable/disabled use isWebDormant
body_excerpt: `handleSend` (ChatShell.tsx ~line 432) still short-circuits on `!isFocused` for any platform. But `editable` and the send button `disabled` now check `isWebDormant` (always `false` on native).
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/session/ChatShell.tsx line 452: `if (!isFocused) return;` (all platforms). Line 1033: `editable={!isStreaming && !isWebDormant}` (web-only dormant check). Line 1075: `disabled={!input.trim() || isStreaming || isWebDormant}` (web-only). On native when a screen is mounted but unfocused (modal overlay, lazy:false tab), the input renders as fully interactive (editable=true, disabled=false) but handleSend silently returns — a dead interaction with no user feedback.
suggested_notion_title: Bug: ChatShell native dead interaction — handleSend silently returns when !isFocused but input/button appear active
suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/components/session/ChatShell.tsx has a split guard: handleSend (line 452) short-circuits on `!isFocused` for ALL platforms, but `editable` (line 1033) and send button `disabled` (line 1075) only check `isWebDormant` (web-only, always false on native). On native, when React Navigation keeps a screen mounted-but-unfocused (modal on top, lazy:false tab), the user sees an interactive input and Send button, but tapping Send silently does nothing. Fix: align handleSend guard with isWebDormant, or revert editable/disabled to use isFocused.

title: use-session-streaming.ts: activeContinueRef while-poll race condition with N>=3 concurrent callers
body_excerpt: **File:** `apps/mobile/src/components/session/use-session-streaming.ts`, `continueWithMessage` The `while (activeContinueRef.current) { await activeContinueRef.current; }` pattern serializes two concurrent callers correctly (B waits for A). With three callers, B and C both await the same promise.
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/session/use-session-streaming.ts lines 513-521 still use the `while (activeContinueRef.current) { await activeContinueRef.current; }` polling pattern. When A resolves and sets ref to null, B and C both exit the while loop in the same microtask tick before either sets the ref, allowing concurrent execution. The promise-chaining fix suggested in the finding (`prev.then(() => currentTurn)`) was not applied. The isStreaming UI guard limits user-driven concurrency but programmatic paths (auto-retry at line 1121) remain at risk.
suggested_notion_title: Bug: use-session-streaming.ts activeContinueRef race condition with N>=3 concurrent callers (auto-retry path)
suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/components/session/use-session-streaming.ts continueWithMessage (line 513) uses a while-poll pattern that serializes 2 callers but breaks with N>=3: when caller A resolves, callers B and C both exit the while loop in the same microtask before either sets activeContinueRef, allowing them to proceed concurrently. The isStreaming UI guard prevents user-driven overlap but the auto-retry path (line 1121) can trigger programmatic N>=3. Fix: chain promises through the ref instead of polling — `const prev = activeContinueRef.current ?? Promise.resolve(); activeContinueRef.current = prev.then(() => currentTurn); await prev;`
